name: Restore The Accepted Base And Increase PTX Grouped-Row Depth

summary.name: Restore The Accepted Base And Increase PTX Grouped-Row Depth
summary.hypothesis: The accepted PTX hot-band kernel is still the fastest measured family, but its grouped-row traversal may not be feeding B-tile reuse aggressively enough. Restoring the accepted base and increasing kFixedHotBandPtxGroupedRows from 4 to 8 should keep more adjacent row tiles on the same hot-band B tile before advancing columns, which may reduce scoreboard waste without reopening the failed 64x384 expansion.
summary.expected_bottleneck: launch-order and B-tile reuse inefficiency inside the accepted PTX hot-band traversal
summary.risk: Low-to-moderate risk: the change is isolated, but a larger grouped-row depth can easily help B reuse while hurting A locality.
ranking_notes: The accepted PTX base still beats every attempted variant in this refactor loop, so the next move should be a small perturbation on that path.
Grouped-row depth is the cleanest way to change CTA issue order and B reuse without touching the K-stage structure.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if runtime change stays within noise or if long-scoreboard stalls rise further after the first measurement.
expected_local_changes: Restore the accepted PTX hot-band baseline by undoing the round-2 pivot-row rebalance.
Increase kFixedHotBandPtxGroupedRows from 4 to 8 while keeping the default PTX hot-band launch and 64x96 tail intact.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
